name: jstall
description: JVM diagnostics with JStall (status, flamegraphs, recording & replay) directly in VSCode

jstall.javaPath.description: Absolute path to a Java 17+ executable. Leave empty for auto-detection (JAVA_HOME, PATH, etc.).

jstall.fullDiagnostics.description: Include expensive analyses (VM vitals, metaspace, etc.)

jstall.intelligentFilter.description: Collapse framework internals in stack traces

jstall.noNative.description: Ignore threads without Java stack traces

jstall.top.description: Number of top threads to show

jstall.recordingDir.description: Directory for saving recordings. Relative paths are resolved from workspace root. Use an absolute path to save outside the workspace.

jstall.remote.sshHosts.description: Known SSH connections for JStall Remote (e.g., "user@hostname", "deploy@prod.example.com").

jstall.remote.cfApps.description: Known Cloud Foundry app names for JStall Remote.

jstall.mcp.status.full.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: include expensive analyses (equivalent to --full).

jstall.mcp.status.intelligentFilter.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: collapse framework internals in stack traces (equivalent to --intelligent-filter).

jstall.mcp.status.noNative.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: ignore threads without Java stack traces (equivalent to --no-native).

jstall.mcp.status.keep.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: persist dumps to disk (equivalent to --keep).

jstall.mcp.status.top.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: number of top threads to show (equivalent to --top).

jstall.mcp.status.dumps.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: number of dumps to collect (equivalent to --dumps).

jstall.mcp.status.interval.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_status: interval between dumps, e.g. '5s' (equivalent to --interval). Empty means use jstall default.

jstall.mcp.flamegraph.durationSeconds.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_flamegraph: profiling duration in seconds (equivalent to --duration).

jstall.mcp.flamegraph.event.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_flamegraph: profiling event, e.g. cpu, alloc, lock, wall, itimer (equivalent to --event).

jstall.mcp.flamegraph.interval.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_flamegraph: sampling interval, e.g. '10ms' (equivalent to --interval).

jstall.mcp.record.full.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_record: include expensive diagnostics (equivalent to --full).

jstall.mcp.record.count.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_record: number of interval-based samples to collect (equivalent to --count).

jstall.mcp.record.interval.description: Default for MCP tool jstall_record: interval between samples, e.g. '5s' (equivalent to --interval).

languageModelTools.name: jstall_list_jvms
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all currently running JVM (Java Virtual Machine) processes on the system. Returns their PIDs and main class names. Use this first to find JVMs before running diagnostics. Marks the active JVM if one is tracked.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List running JVM processes and highlight the active one.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs an arbitrary jstall CLI command and returns its text output. Pass any arguments as a string array. Use ["--help"] to discover all available commands, or ["status", "--help"] to see options for a specific command. Examples: ["status", "--full", "12345"], ["deadlock", "12345"], ["threads", "--no-native", "12345"], ["most-work", "--top=5", "12345"], ["waiting-threads", "12345"], ["dependency-graph", "12345"], ["vm-vitals", "12345"], ["gc-heap-info", "12345"], ["compiler-queue", "12345"], ["processes", "12345"], ["jvm-support", "12345"], ["status", "recording.zip"], ["record", "summary", "recording.zip"]. For flamegraph or recording creation prefer jstall_flamegraph / jstall_record tools instead (they handle file paths automatically).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run any JStall CLI command with explicit arguments.

args.description: Arguments to pass to jstall, e.g. ["--help"] or ["status", "--full", "12345"].

languageModelTools.name: jstall_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs JStall status diagnostics on a JVM process, all JVM processes, or a recording ZIP. Returns thread analysis, deadlock detection, top threads, and other diagnostic information. Supports all status flags. Set pid to "all" to analyze all JVMs. If no PID or recording is specified, uses the currently active JVM. When the output contains deadlocks or problematic threads, use jstall_reveal_code_batch to highlight all offending frames at once (with severity 'error' for deadlocks, 'warning' for blocked threads). Include explanations describing what each thread holds/awaits.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run full JVM status diagnostics on a live process or recording ZIP.

pid.description: PID of the JVM process to analyze, or "all" to analyze all JVMs. If omitted, uses the active JVM.

recordingZip.description: Path to a recording ZIP — analyze a recording instead of a live JVM.

full.description: Run all analyses including expensive ones (VM vitals, metaspace, etc.).

intelligentFilter.description: Use intelligent stack trace filtering (collapses internal frames, focuses on application code).

noNative.description: Ignore threads without stack traces (typically native/system threads).

top.description: Number of top threads to show (default: 3).

languageModelTools.name: jstall_flamegraph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Captures a CPU profiling flamegraph for a JVM process (or from a recording ZIP) and returns the path to the generated HTML file. If no PID is specified, uses the currently active JVM. Supports cpu, alloc, lock, wall, itimer events.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Capture and return a flamegraph HTML for a JVM or recording.

pid.description: PID of the JVM process to profile. If omitted, uses the active JVM.

event.description: Profiling event: cpu, alloc, lock, wall, itimer (default: cpu).

recordingZip.description: Path to a recording ZIP — generate flamegraph from replay instead of a live JVM.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_record
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Records JVM diagnostics over time into a ZIP file for later analysis. Captures thread dumps, heap info, and profiling data. Set pid to "all" to record all JVMs on the system. If no PID is specified, uses the currently active JVM. The returned ZIP can be analyzed with jstall_run (e.g. ["status", "<path>"]) or jstall_flamegraph (recordingZip parameter).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create a JVM diagnostics recording ZIP for later analysis.

pid.description: PID of the JVM process to record, or "all" to record all JVMs. If omitted, uses the active JVM.

count.description: Number of interval-based samples to collect (default: 2).

languageModelTools.name: jstall_remote
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs a jstall command against a remote JVM via SSH or Cloud Foundry (CF). Use type "ssh" with a target like "user@hostname" for SSH connections, or type "cf" with a Cloud Foundry app name. First use command "list" to discover running JVMs on the remote machine (returns PIDs and main class names). Then use command "status" with the PID to get full diagnostics. When the output contains deadlocks or problematic threads, use jstall_reveal_code_batch to highlight all offending frames at once (with severity 'error' for deadlocks, 'warning' for blocked threads, and explanations). Other supported commands: threads, deadlock, most-work, waiting-threads, dependency-graph, vm-vitals, gc-heap-info, flame, record create. Pass the PID and flags via args, e.g. args: ["12345"] or ["--full", "12345"].
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run JStall diagnostics on a remote JVM via SSH or Cloud Foundry.

type.description: Connection type: "ssh" for SSH or "cf" for Cloud Foundry.

target.description: SSH host (e.g. "user@hostname") or Cloud Foundry app name.

command.description: The jstall command to run. Use "list" to discover remote JVMs first, then "status" for diagnostics. Other commands: "threads", "deadlock", "most-work", "waiting-threads", "dependency-graph", "vm-vitals", "gc-heap-info", "flame", "record create".

languageModelTools.name: jstall_reveal_code
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens a Java source file in the editor and highlights a specific line or range. Use this after jstall_status finds issues — pass the fully-qualified class name and line number from the stack trace. ALWAYS provide 'explanation' and 'severity'. Use severity 'error' (red) for deadlocks, 'warning' (yellow) for blocked/waiting threads, 'info' (blue) for general navigation. Use 'endLine' to highlight an entire synchronized block or method. For deadlocks, use viewColumn 'one' and 'beside' to show both frames side by side. For multiple locations, prefer jstall_reveal_code_batch instead of multiple individual calls.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Navigate to a Java class and highlight a specific line or range.

className.description: Fully-qualified Java class name (e.g. 'com.example.OrderService'). May include ':lineNumber' suffix (e.g. 'com.example.OrderService:42').

lineNumber.description: 1-based line number to highlight and jump to.

endLine.description: Optional 1-based end line to highlight a range (e.g. an entire synchronized block). If omitted, only the single line is highlighted.

message.description: Optional message to display as a VS Code notification.

explanation.description: Inline annotation displayed beside the highlighted line (e.g. 'Thread-A holds lockA, waiting for lockB'). ALWAYS provide this.

severity.description: Highlight color: 'error' (red) for deadlocks, 'warning' (yellow) for blocked/waiting threads, 'info' (blue, default) for general.

viewColumn.description: Editor column to open in. Use 'beside' or explicit 'one'/'two'/'three'. For deadlocks, use 'one' + 'beside' to show side by side.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_reveal_code_batch
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reveals and highlights multiple Java code locations in a single call. Preferred over calling jstall_reveal_code multiple times. Each location can have its own severity, explanation, line range, and viewColumn. Ideal for deadlocks (show both frames), circular dependencies (show all participants), or top-N hot threads.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Navigate to and highlight multiple Java code locations at once.

locations.description: Array of code locations to reveal and highlight.

endLine.description: Optional end line for range highlighting.

message.description: Optional notification message shown after all locations are revealed.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_clear_highlights
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes all JStall code highlights that were previously added by jstall_reveal_code or jstall_reveal_code_batch. Call this after the user has reviewed the highlighted code or before highlighting a new set of lines for a different issue.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Remove all JStall line highlights from the editor.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_dependency_graph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Visualizes thread lock dependencies as a Mermaid flowchart diagram rendered inline in the chat. Shows which threads are waiting on locks held by other threads, with deadlock cycles highlighted in red. Returns the raw JStall dependency-graph text. Use this after jstall_status detects deadlocks or contention.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Show a visual Mermaid diagram of thread lock dependencies.

pid.description: PID of the JVM process to analyze, or "all". If omitted, uses the active JVM.

recordingZip.description: Path to a recording ZIP to analyze instead of a live JVM.

intelligentFilter.description: Collapse framework internals in stack traces.

languageModelTools.name: jstall_run_profiled
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs a Java app with async-profiler attached from start. Profiles the full lifecycle (startup, short-lived apps). Returns process output + path to profile file (collapsed stacks by default — use .html for flamegraph, .jfr for JFR). Events: cpu, alloc, lock, wall, itimer.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run a Java app with async-profiler from the start.

javaArgs.description: Arguments to pass to the java command (everything after 'java'). Examples: ["-jar", "target/app.jar", "arg1"], ["-cp", "classes", "com.example.Main", "--flag"].

event.description: Profiling event: cpu, alloc, lock, wall, itimer (default: cpu).

interval.description: Sampling interval, e.g. '10ms' or '1000000' (in nanoseconds). Default depends on event.

outputFile.description: Path for the profile output file. Defaults to a temp .collapsed file whose content is returned directly. Use .html for flamegraph, .jfr for JFR.

cwd.description: Working directory for the Java process. Defaults to the workspace root.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
